title: Fix check-ai-pr paired ownership for release files

intent.problem: Release file changes on the main branch need a paired archived Work Item so check-ai-pr recognizes them as owned PR diff paths.
constraints: Keep the release metadata and fixed-version examples aligned on the same published version.
Do not change check-ai-pr semantics; add the missing Work Item ownership evidence instead.
intent.rationale: A release Work Item archive pair can claim the release metadata edits and allow the PR ownership check to pass without weakening validation.

sources.reason: Canonical public release metadata that must match the published tag.
Installer default ref must stay aligned with the published release version.
Installation docs include a fixed version example that should match the new release.
Upgrade docs include a fixed version example that should match the new release.
Example install commands hardcode the published release tag.
Regression coverage should reflect the published release tag used by the distribution check.
Defines the GitHub Actions workflow that should exercise compatibility instead of local ad-hoc runs.

riskAssessment.reason: Bumping the published release tag requires consistent updates across release metadata, installer defaults, fixed-version docs, and the release distribution fixture.

executionDecision.reason: The version bump is mechanical once the published release tag is chosen.

preReviewWarnings: Keep the release tag identical across release.json, install.sh, docs, examples, and tests.
Do not run compatibility locally; only observe the GitHub Actions result.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A release Work Item archive pair covers the release metadata edits on this branch.
check-ai-pr passes for the complete PR diff.
The archived Summary remains valid after finish and archive.
guidelines: Keep release metadata synchronized across all fixed-version references.
Preserve the PR ownership check semantics; do not weaken validation rules.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
